examples.identity: Instructor marks normal force, gravity, and friction on an inclined-plane example.
rules: Draw one force arrow per physical force
Resolve gravity into components parallel and perpendicular to the plane
Do not invent a force in the direction of motion
examples.instructor_annotation: Instructor circles the force arrows and labels the axes along and perpendicular to the plane.
examples.exam_answer: Start from the free-body diagram, resolve forces, then write equations along each axis.
examples.trap: Do not draw both gravity and a separate downhill 'motion force'.
examples.memory: Forces first, components second, equations third.
